name: Nautobot Network Source of Truth API
what_it_does: Open-source Network Source of Truth (NSoT) and network automation platform with REST and GraphQL APIs. Fork of NetBox with added automation plugins, Jobs framework, and extensibility for network-as-code workflows.
use_cases: Querying network device inventory, IP address assignments, and circuit data for automation agents
Triggering network configuration jobs (backup, compliance, provision) via Jobs API
Managing IPAM (IP Address Management) allocations programmatically
Syncing network state from external systems to maintain source-of-truth
Building network automation workflows using GraphQL for efficient multi-resource queries
not_for: Non-network IT teams — Nautobot is purpose-built for network engineering
Simple IP tracking without network automation requirements (simpler IPAM tools exist)
Organizations already invested in NetBox without need for automation platform features
best_when: An agent needs structured network data (devices, IPs, cables, circuits) combined with automation job execution across network devices.
avoid_when: You only need IP address management without network automation — use simpler IPAM tools.

auth.notes: Token-based authentication. API tokens created per user with optional object-level permissions. GraphQL and REST API share the same auth token. Admin users can create service-account tokens.

pricing.paid_tiers: Network to Code (creators) offers commercial support and managed Nautobot
pricing.notes: Nautobot is Apache 2.0 open-source maintained by Network to Code. Extensive plugin ecosystem. Network to Code offers commercial support plans and consulting.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: REST API documented via Swagger/OpenAPI. GraphQL schema self-documenting. Error responses are JSON. Jobs API for automation tasks is well-documented. pynautobot SDK simplifies Python integration.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Network data creation is idempotent by design — duplicate detection by natural keys. REST API supports update-or-create patterns.
known_agent_gotchas: Jobs API requires celery worker — async job execution with polling for results
Plugin ecosystem varies in quality — test plugin APIs thoroughly before relying on them
GraphQL vs REST: GraphQL is more efficient for complex queries; REST for simple CRUD
Object permissions must be configured carefully — default is all-or-nothing by model type

security_score_components.security_notes: Apache 2.0 open-source — auditable. Token-based auth with object-level permissions. TLS configurable (not default in dev mode). Network data is sensitive — production deployments should enforce TLS.
